Implement the following plan:

# Fix Profile Share Route Bugs

## Context
The profile share feature was implemented but has 3 bugs:
1. **Wrong redirect URL** — uses `/@username` but client routes use `/u/username`
2. **SVG text not rendering** — Sharp's librsvg doesn't support CSS `<style>` blocks with classes or `rgba()` fill values in SVG, so name/username/bio appear invisible
3. **Background image may silently fail** — errors are swallowed; also need to handle gradient-only backgrounds

## Bug Details

### Bug 1: Wrong URL pattern (`/@` → `/u/`)
**File:** `server/server.js` line ~587
```js
// CURRENT (wrong):
const clientProfileUrl = `${SITE_URL}/@${encodeURIComponent(username)}`;
// CORRECT:
const clientProfileUrl = `${SITE_URL}/u/${encodeURIComponent(username)}`;
```
The client router (`App.jsx:101`) defines `path='/u/:username'`. There is no `/@username` route. The `og:url`, canonical, and redirect all point to a non-existent route.

### Bug 2: SVG text invisible in Sharp
**File:** `server/server.js` lines ~543-555

Sharp renders SVG via librsvg which has limited CSS support:
- `<style>` blocks with CSS classes → **not reliably supported**
- `rgba()` as SVG `fill` value → **not supported** in librsvg (SVG 1.1)

**Fix:** Replace CSS classes with inline SVG attributes. Replace `rgba()` with `fill` + `fill-opacity`:
```xml
<!-- BEFORE (broken) -->
<style>.name { font-family: sans-serif; fill: rgba(255,255,255,0.7); }</style>
<text class="name">...</text>

<!-- AFTER (works) -->
<text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="48" font-weight="700" fill="white" text-anchor="middle">...</text>
<text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="28" fill="white" fill-opacity="0.7" text-anchor="middle">...</text>
```

### Bug 3: Background extraction needs gradient handling
**File:** `server/server.js` lines ~462-478

The `background` column stores either:
- **Image:** `{ backgroundImage: "url(https://...)", backgroundSize: "cover", ... }`
- **Gradient:** `{ background: "#2A7B9B", backgroundImage: "linear-gradient(90deg, ...)" }`

Current code extracts `url()` from `backgroundImage` — this correctly fails for gradients (falls through to dark fallback). But we should also render the gradient as the base layer when there's no image URL.

## Changes

### 1. Fix redirect URL in HTML share route
**File:** `server/server.js`
- Change `/@${encodeURIComponent(username)}` → `/u/${encodeURIComponent(username)}`
- Affects: `clientProfileUrl`, which feeds `og:url`, canonical, meta refresh, JS redirect

### 2. Fix SVG text rendering in image share route
**File:** `server/server.js`
- Remove the `<style>` block entirely
- Use inline SVG attributes on each `<text>` element:
  - `font-family`, `font-size`, `font-weight` as attributes
  - `fill="white"` with `fill-opacity="0.7"` instead of `rgba()`
  - Keep `text-anchor="middle"` as attribute

### 3. Render gradient backgrounds as base layer
**File:** `server/server.js`
- When `backgroundImage` contains `linear-gradient(...)` (no `url()`), render the gradient as an SVG base layer instead of the dark fallback
- Parse the gradient string and create an SVG `<linearGradient>` — OR simpler: just use the `background` (solid color) property as a flat color fill when no image URL is found, since librsvg also has limited gradient parsing
- Simplest approach: if `bgStyle.background` is a hex color and no image URL, use that as the base layer color

## File Modified
| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `server/server.js` | Fix URL pattern, fix SVG inline styles, handle gradient backgrounds |

## Verification
1. Visit `http://localhost:3000/share/u/{username}/image` — should see 1200x630 JPEG with visible name, @handle, bio text
2. Visit `http://localhost:3000/share/u/{username}` — HTML page should redirect to `/u/{username}` (not `/@username`)
3. Test with user who has: background image, gradient background, no background
4. Paste share URL into Facebook/Twitter debugger to confirm OG preview works


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: C:\Users\johnm\.claude\projects\C--Users-johnm-iskrib-v3\e23e506a-5846-4b9d-929e-bdefbf38e5bc.jsonl

# Fix Profile Share Route Bugs

## Context
The profile share feature was implemented but has 3 bugs:
1. **Wrong redirect URL** — uses `/@username` but client routes use `/u/username`
2. **SVG text not rendering** — Sharp's librsvg doesn't support CSS `<style>` blocks with classes or `rgba()` fill values in SVG, so name/username/bio appear invisible
3. **Background image may silently fail** — errors are swallowed; also need to handle gradient-only backgrounds

## Bug Details

### Bug 1: Wrong URL pattern (`/@` → `/u/`)
**File:** `server/server.js` line ~587
```js
// CURRENT (wrong):
const clientProfileUrl = `${SITE_URL}/@${encodeURIComponent(username)}`;
// CORRECT:
const clientProfileUrl = `${SITE_URL}/u/${encodeURIComponent(username)}`;
```
The client router (`App.jsx:101`) defines `path='/u/:username'`. There is no `/@username` route. The `og:url`, canonical, and redirect all point to a non-existent route.

### Bug 2: SVG text invisible in Sharp
**File:** `server/server.js` lines ~543-555

Sharp renders SVG via librsvg which has limited CSS support:
- `<style>` blocks with CSS classes → **not reliably supported**
- `rgba()` as SVG `fill` value → **not supported** in librsvg (SVG 1.1)

**Fix:** Replace CSS classes with inline SVG attributes. Replace `rgba()` with `fill` + `fill-opacity`:
```xml
<!-- BEFORE (broken) -->
<style>.name { font-family: sans-serif; fill: rgba(255,255,255,0.7); }</style>
<text class="name">...</text>

<!-- AFTER (works) -->
<text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="48" font-weight="700" fill="white" text-anchor="middle">...</text>
<text font-family="sans-serif" font-size="28" fill="white" fill-opacity="0.7" text-anchor="middle">...</text>
```

### Bug 3: Background extraction needs gradient handling
**File:** `server/server.js` lines ~462-478

The `background` column stores either:
- **Image:** `{ backgroundImage: "url(https://...)", backgroundSize: "cover", ... }`
- **Gradient:** `{ background: "#2A7B9B", backgroundImage: "linear-gradient(90deg, ...)" }`

Current code extracts `url()` from `backgroundImage` — this correctly fails for gradients (falls through to dark fallback). But we should also render the gradient as the base layer when there's no image URL.

## Changes

### 1. Fix redirect URL in HTML share route
**File:** `server/server.js`
- Change `/@${encodeURIComponent(username)}` → `/u/${encodeURIComponent(username)}`
- Affects: `clientProfileUrl`, which feeds `og:url`, canonical, meta refresh, JS redirect

### 2. Fix SVG text rendering in image share route
**File:** `server/server.js`
- Remove the `<style>` block entirely
- Use inline SVG attributes on each `<text>` element:
  - `font-family`, `font-size`, `font-weight` as attributes
  - `fill="white"` with `fill-opacity="0.7"` instead of `rgba()`
  - Keep `text-anchor="middle"` as attribute

### 3. Render gradient backgrounds as base layer
**File:** `server/server.js`
- When `backgroundImage` contains `linear-gradient(...)` (no `url()`), render the gradient as an SVG base layer instead of the dark fallback
- Parse the gradient string and create an SVG `<linearGradient>` — OR simpler: just use the `background` (solid color) property as a flat color fill when no image URL is found, since librsvg also has limited gradient parsing
- Simplest approach: if `bgStyle.background` is a hex color and no image URL, use that as the base layer color

## File Modified
| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `server/server.js` | Fix URL pattern, fix SVG inline styles, handle gradient backgrounds |

## Verification
1. Visit `http://localhost:3000/share/u/{username}/image` — should see 1200x630 JPEG with visible name, @handle, bio text
2. Visit `http://localhost:3000/share/u/{username}` — HTML page should redirect to `/u/{username}` (not `/@username`)
3. Test with user who has: background image, gradient background, no background
4. Paste share URL into Facebook/Twitter debugger to confirm OG preview works

content.text: Good — `stop-color` is already an attribute (not `style="stop-color:..."`). Now let me verify the text SVG and redirect URL look correct:

All three fixes are applied. Here's a summary:

**Bug 1 — Wrong redirect URL** (line 588): Changed `/@` to `/u/` so the `clientProfileUrl` matches the actual client route `/u/:username`. This fixes `og:url`, canonical, meta refresh, and JS redirect.

**Bug 2 — SVG text not rendering** (lines 550-555): Removed the `<style>` block with CSS classes entirely. Each `<text>` element now uses inline SVG attributes (`font-family`, `font-size`, `font-weight`, `fill`, `fill-opacity`) instead of class selectors and `rgba()` values — both of which librsvg doesn't support.

Also fixed the dark overlay (line 510): changed `fill="rgba(0,0,0,0.55)"` to `fill="black" fill-opacity="0.55"`.

**Bug 3 — Gradient background handling** (lines 486-506): When there's no background image URL, the code now checks if `bgStyle.background` is a hex color (e.g. `#2A7B9B`). If so, it uses that as a solid color fill for the base layer. Otherwise it falls back to the dark gradient. Also fixed `stop-color` in the fallback gradient to use attributes instead of inline `style`.

content.thinking:
The user is showing Facebook's sharing debugger results. The key issues are:

1. **Canonical URL is `https://iskrib.com/`** instead of `https://iskrib.com/u/loren-the-great` — This means the `og:url` is resolving to just the root. Facebook followed the `og:url` to `https://iskrib.com/u/loren-the-great`, and THAT page's OG tags have `og:url` set to `https://iskrib.com/` (the homepage).

2. **Response Code 206** — Partial content, which is unusual.


3. **fb:app_id missing** — This is a warning but not critical.

The real issue is that Facebook's crawler follows `og:url`, so when it hits the share page at `/share/u/loren-the-great`, it gets redirected to the React SPA at `/u/loren-the-great`. Since the SPA doesn't execute JavaScript for crawlers, Facebook sees the default homepage OG tags instead of the dynamically set profile tags, causing the canonical URL to point back to the root.

Looking at the debug output more carefully, the `og:url` is showing `https://iskrib.com/` when it should be set to the full profile URL, which suggests the dynamic OG tags aren't being rendered in the initial HTML that Facebook receives. I see the issue now—Facebook fetches the share page and reads its OG tags, but the `og:url` is pointing to the client profile URL, which makes Facebook follow that link to the SPA. When it does, it picks up the default OG tags from `index.html` instead of the correct ones from the share page. The solution is to set `og:url` to the share URL itself so Facebook doesn't redirect and grab the wrong metadata.

content.text:
The problem is clear: Facebook follows the `og:url` to `https://iskrib.com/u/loren-the-great`, which is the React SPA. The SPA's `index.html` has default OG tags with `og:url: https://iskrib.com/`, so Facebook picks up those tags instead of the share page's tags.

The fix: `og:url` should point to the share page itself (so Facebook doesn't chase it to the SPA), while the redirect/meta-refresh still sends human visitors to the client app.

The fix: `og:url` and `canonical` should point to the share page itself. The meta-refresh and JS redirect still send humans to the client app.

content.text:
Here's what changed and why:

**Root cause:** Facebook's crawler follows `og:url`. When it pointed to `https://iskrib.com/u/loren-the-great` (the SPA), Facebook fetched that page and found the SPA's default OG tags with `og:url: https://iskrib.com/`, which overwrote everything.

**Fixes applied:**

1. **`og:url` and `canonical` now point to the share page itself** (`sharePageUrl` = `/share/u/username`) instead of the client SPA URL. This keeps Facebook on the page that has the correct OG tags — it won't chase to the SPA.

2. **Added a 2-second delay to the redirect** (`content="2;url=..."` and `setTimeout(..., 2000)`). This gives Facebook's crawler time to read the OG tags before the page redirects. An instant redirect (`content="0"`) was causing the 206 partial content response — the redirect was racing the scrape.

3. **Human visitors still redirect** to `/u/username` via meta-refresh and JS — they just wait 2 seconds first.

**Note:** The post share route (line 762) has the same `og:url` pointing to the SPA issue. Want me to fix that one too?
